title: Search is really slow
body: The search feature used to be fast but now it takes forever. Sometimes it takes 10-15 seconds to get results. I have a lot of tasks but this seems excessive. Running TaskFlow on my work laptop.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
Thanks for reporting this. A few things that would help me narrow this down:

When did you first notice the slowdown? Did it coincide with anything specific — a TaskFlow update, a large import of tasks, or any other change you can recall?

Also, roughly how many tasks are in your workspace? (You can check this in **Settings → Workspace Stats** if you're unsure of the exact count.)

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: It started about 2 weeks ago. I'm pretty sure it lines up with when I upgraded to v2.3 — I don't remember it being slow before that. I haven't done any big imports or anything like that recently. As for task count, I've been using TaskFlow for about 2 years now so I've got around 5,000 tasks in there.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
Thanks for confirming the timeline — a regression tied to v2.3 with 5,000 tasks is a solid starting point.

I want to understand the slowdown more precisely:

1. Is **every** search slow, or only certain kinds? For example, does searching for a single common word like "meeting" take just as long as searching for something rare or specific?
2. During those 10-15 seconds, what do you see — does the app freeze completely (can't click anything), or do you see a loading spinner/progress indicator while it works?

This will help us figure out whether the search index itself is the problem or whether it's something else in how v2.3 handles the query.

comments.author: reporter
comments.body:
Good question — I just tested this. Searching for a common word like "meeting" is slow no matter what. But I noticed something interesting: if I search by task title it's still fast, like it used to be. It's only when I'm searching across task descriptions that it crawls. Some of my tasks have really long descriptions (I paste meeting notes into them, sometimes pretty lengthy), not sure if that matters.

As for the UI, it doesn't completely freeze — I can still click around, but the search area just sits there with a loading spinner for 10-15 seconds before results finally show up. I can see my CPU fan spin up too, if that helps.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body: [RESOLVED] {"title":"Search across task descriptions regressed to ~10-15s in v2.3 (title search unaffected)","problem":"After upgrading to TaskFlow v2.3, searching across task descriptions takes 10-15 seconds with high CPU usage. Searching by task title remains fast. The reporter has ~5,000 tasks, many with lengthy descriptions (pasted meeting notes). The slowdown is consistent regardless of search term specificity.","root_cause_hypothesis":"v2.3 likely introduced a change to how description search is executed — most probably the description field lost its search index, the index is no longer being built/maintained, or the search path was changed from an indexed query to a full-text scan. The fact that title search remains fast while description search is uniformly slow (regardless of query term) strongly suggests the description field is being scanned sequentially rather than looked up via index. The CPU spike corroborates a brute-force scan over large text blobs.","reproduction_steps":["Set up a TaskFlow v2.3 workspace with a substantial number of tasks (~5,000) including tasks with long description fields","Perform a search using the description search mode (not title-only) for any common term like 'meeting'","Observe 10-15 second delay with CPU spike before results appear","Compare by switching to title-only search and confirming it returns results quickly"],"environment":"TaskFlow v2.3, work laptop (specific OS not confirmed), ~5,000 tasks accumulated over 2 years, many tasks containing lengthy pasted meeting notes in descriptions","severity":"medium","impact":"Affects any user with a non-trivial number of tasks who searches by description content. Workaround exists (search by title only), but users who rely on description search — especially those with rich task descriptions — are significantly impacted in daily workflow. Likely affects more users as task count grows.","recommended_fix":"Diff the search implementation between v2.2 and v2.3, focusing on: (1) whether description fields are still indexed for search, (2) whether the search query path for descriptions changed (e.g., from indexed lookup to sequential scan or regex match), (3) whether index rebuild/migration runs correctly on upgrade. Check if a search index migration was added in v2.3 that may not be executing properly, leaving description fields unindexed.","proposed_test_case":"Performance test: seed a workspace with 5,000+ tasks (including tasks with descriptions >1KB), execute a description search, and assert results return within an acceptable threshold (e.g., <1 second). Run this test against both v2.2 (baseline) and v2.3 to confirm the regression and validate the fix.","information_gaps":["Exact OS and hardware specs of the reporter's work laptop (unlikely to change the fix approach)","Whether other v2.3 users with large workspaces are experiencing the same issue","Whether the issue scales linearly with task count or description length specifically","Server-side vs client-side — whether TaskFlow search runs locally or involves a backend (CPU fan spin suggests local)"]}
